questions: Was the version correctly bumped to 1.3.4 in package.json?
Was the version correctly bumped to 1.3.4 in both locations in package-lock.json?
Do typecheck and tests pass after the version bump?
Were any files modified beyond package.json and package-lock.json?

answers.answer: Yes. package.json line 3 now reads "version": "1.3.4". Verified via node -e script that parses the file and checks the version field.
evidence: node -e script output: OK: version is 1.3.4
Direct file read confirms package.json line 3: "version": "1.3.4"

answers.answer: Yes. package-lock.json line 3 (root level) and line 9 (packages[""] level) both read "version": "1.3.4". Verified via node -e script that parses both locations.
evidence: node -e script output: OK: root: 1.3.4 pkg: 1.3.4
Direct file read confirms package-lock.json lines 3 and 9 both show "version": "1.3.4"

answers.answer: Yes. npx tsc --noEmit exited with code 0 (no type errors). npm test exited with code 0 with 51 tests passing and 0 failures.
evidence: npm test: 51 pass, 0 fail, 0 skipped, duration 87ms

answers.answer: No. Only package.json and package-lock.json were modified. The edits used targeted replace operations that only changed the version field from 1.3.3 to 1.3.4. Grep for 1.3.3 in *.json confirmed no stale references remain in project files.
evidence: Grep for 1.3.3 in *.json: no matches in package.json or package-lock.json
Only 3 targeted string replacements were applied across 2 files

statement: Version bump from 1.3.3 to 1.3.4 applied successfully to package.json (line 3) and package-lock.json (lines 3, 9). Typecheck and all 51 tests pass. No source code, workflow, or config files were modified. The change enables the existing auto-tag + publish pipeline to create v1.3.4 and publish to npm on merge to main.
